SEARCH
Module geomed.app
Package geomed.app.test

Class SimulationDataGenerator

java.lang.Object
geomed.app.test.SimulationDataGenerator
public class SimulationDataGenerator extends Object
Utility data factory class responsible for provisioning randomized mock test structures.

This factory generates simulation instances of Hospital units, VictimIncident occurrences, and matching interconnected RoadEdge paths. By design, this class is entirely decoupled and agnostic of any visual toolkit or graphic interface subsystem, rendering it fully reusable across lightweight command-line Console runtimes and full JavaFX GUI layout dashboard components.

Version:
1.0

  • Method Details

    • generateRandomHospitals

      public static List<Hospital> generateRandomHospitals(int count, int startId)
      Generates an array list of randomized hospital structures.

      Each simulated entity is configured with:

      • A random maximum intake capacity value constrained within the range [10, 100].
      • Planar geometric placement layout coordinates bound inside safe operational zones (X: 50 to 700 px, Y: 50 to 600 px).
      • A default fallback MedicalSpecialty.GENERAL qualification combined with a secondary, randomly selected medical discipline priority assignment.
      Parameters:
      count - the specific quantity of structural hospital instances to instantiate
      startId - the baseline auto-incrementing unique identifier index to start numbering from
      Returns:
      a compiled list filled with unique, randomly distributed Hospital data model nodes

    • generateRandomIncidents

      public static List<VictimIncident> generateRandomIncidents(int count, int startCount, List<Hospital> currentHospitals)
      Generates a batch collection of randomized emergency victim incidents.

      Every simulated incident event is seeded with:

      • A padded alphanumeric structural identifier format string (e.g., INC-R-001).
      • A single specialized primary medical triage emergency requirement category type.
      • A 20% mathematical probability of configuring a fixed preferred destination hospital matching an item from the supplied available hubs list.
      Parameters:
      count - the explicit quantity of mock victim emergency incidents to create
      startCount - the numerical tracking offset used to format ordered serial tracking identifier tags
      currentHospitals - the active cluster of baseline hospital nodes currently registered in the topology map
      Returns:
      a collection array consisting of randomized, unmapped VictimIncident emergency contexts

    • generateRandomRoads

      public static void generateRandomRoads(MapManager mapManager, int nbRoads)
      Injects a randomized, multi-weighted grid network of roads interconnecting registered active hospital nodes.

      Traffic Congestion Probability Distribution Model:

      • There is a 30% statistical probability that a path experiences congestion issues. The resulting traffic factor metric dynamically scales within the range [1.5, 4.0).
      • The remaining 70% of connections map as standard fluid traffic links, carrying a baseline traffic factor value of exactly 1.0.

      Links connecting a single hospital node back onto itself are intercepted and discarded to prevent infinite routing self-loops.

      Parameters:
      mapManager - the underlying target central core orchestrator system context instance to inject roads into
      nbRoads - the total target quantity of cross-grid connective road segments to generate
